rationale: Chitturi et al. (2007) is used in the conceptual development, hypothesis logic, and interpretation of results, not just as a background mention. The citing paper repeatedly invokes its function-before-hedonics premise to motivate predictions about how incongruent form is evaluated under functional versus experiential positioning. The authors then test related utilitarian and hedonic mediation patterns and discuss their contribution as building on that prior line of work with a new boundary condition.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Chitturi et al. (2007) as a key prior account that hedonic preference depends on functional assurance, and extends this logic to incongruent product form and positioning strategy. It shows that positioning changes whether incongruent form raises hedonic benefit perceptions or lowers utilitarian benefit perceptions, producing opposite evaluation outcomes.
